mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in FEDFUNDS lowers discount rates and refinancing stress, which supports equity multiples and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when policy is easing rather than tightening.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of months with a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or if rolling 2-year rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above a recent threshold signals weakening household income and demand, which reduces earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not show negative 20-day SPX returns more often than positive ones, or if the conditional average return is not below the unconditional average by at least 1 percentage point annualized.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous acceleration in RSAFS and PAYEMS indicates broadening nominal demand and hiring, which improves near-term revenue expectations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising RSAFS and PAYEMS does not outperform either series alone, or if 5-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 60% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in INDPRO after prior weakness reflects improving industrial supply utilization and earnings cyclicality, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days when activity is recovering from a slump.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises for 2 consecutive months after a 3-month decline
failure_condition: Reject if the trigger is followed by negative or flat 60-day SPX returns on average, or if the hit rate falls below 50% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST falls while UNRATE rises, the housing-demand slowdown and labor deterioration jointly signal a broad cyclical contraction that depresses S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined HOUST-down/UNRATE-up state does not produce lower 20-day SPX returns than either signal alone, or if the conditional probability of negative returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in CPIAUCSL inflation with a delayed FEDFUNDS response raises real-rate and discount-rate pressure, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates above its 6-month average while FEDFUNDS is unchanged or rising
failure_condition: Reject if high-inflation, non-easing episodes do not underperform the unconditional 60-day SPX baseline, or if the signal loses significance after controlling for FEDFUNDS alone.

mechanism_narrative: In a labor-market stress regime, PAYEMS declines combined with UNRATE increases capture a falling payroll-to-unemployment balance that weakens risk appetite and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-down and UNRATE-up condition does not produce a negative 5-day return edge, or if the effect disappears once PAYEMS is replaced by its sign alone.

mechanism_narrative: A negative GDP growth surprise in GDPC1 matters most during recovery phases because investors reprice cyclicals sharply when prior growth was expected to normalize; under that regime, weaker GDPC1 implies negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 prints below its prior quarter and below a 2-quarter moving average
failure_condition: Reject if below-trend GDPC1 quarters are not followed by below-median 60-day SPX returns, or if the conditional edge is not present specifically in recovery phases.
